---
title: "Day 3: Florence - Duomo & Uffizi"
subtitle: "First Full Day - Renaissance Art"
date: "2026-06-06"
day-of-week: "Friday"
location: "Florence"
categories: [itinerary, florence, museums, art]
---

## Overview

First full day in Florence! Today focuses on the city's two most iconic experiences: the magnificent Duomo complex and the world-class Uffizi Gallery.

**Highlights:** Duomo dome climb, Uffizi Gallery, Ponte Vecchio, Piazzale Michelangelo sunset

---

## Morning: Duomo Complex (8 AM - 12 PM)

### Breakfast (7:30-8 AM)

**Café Breakfast:**
- Find local café near hotel
- Order: "Un cappuccino e un cornetto, per favore"
- Stand at bar (cheaper) or sit (more expensive but relaxing)
- Cost: €3-5
- Watch Florentines start their day

### Duomo Complex

**See:** [Museums Research - Duomo](../research/museums.qmd#duomo-complex)

**What's Included in Combo Ticket (€30):**
- Cathedral nave (free, but combo ticket needed for other sites)
- Brunelleschi's Dome climb ⭐ **MUST DO**
- Giotto's Bell Tower (Campanile)
- Baptistery
- Opera Museum
- Crypt

**Priority Order:**

**1. Dome Climb (463 steps) - FIRST**
- **Timed entry required** - check your booking time
- Go early before heat of day
- No elevator, narrow passages (not for claustrophobic)
- Incredible views of Florence from top
- See Vasari's Last Judgment frescoes up close
- Allow 45-60 minutes

**2. Cathedral Interior**
- Free entry to nave
- Massive scale, relatively simple interior
- Look up at dome from inside
- 20-30 minutes

**3. Baptistery**
- Ghiberti's "Gates of Paradise" bronze doors
- Byzantine mosaics in dome
- 20 minutes

**4. Bell Tower (Optional)**
- 414 steps, different views than dome
- Skip if tired from dome climb

---

## Afternoon: Uffizi Gallery (2 PM - 5 PM)

### Lunch (12:30-1:30 PM)

**Options:**
- **Mercato Centrale** (upstairs food court) - Quick, good, cheap
- **All'Antico Vinaio** - Famous sandwiches (Via dei Neri)
- Any trattoria near Duomo

**Tip:** Light lunch - you'll be standing at Uffizi for 3 hours

### Uffizi Gallery ⭐ **PRIORITY**

**See:** [Museums Research - Uffizi](../research/museums.qmd#uffizi-gallery)

**Booking:** Check your timed entry - arrive 15 min early

**Strategy:**
- This is HUGE - pace yourself
- Focus on highlights, don't try to see everything
- Take breaks on benches

**Must-See Rooms:**

**Room 10-14: Botticelli**
- *Birth of Venus*
- *Primavera*
- The reason you're here

**Room 15: Leonardo da Vinci**
- *Annunciation*
- *Adoration of the Magi*

**Room 35: Michelangelo**
- *Doni Tondo* (Holy Family)

**Room 90: Caravaggio**
- *Medusa*
- *Bacchus*

**Also Great:**
- Raphael, Titian, Giotto, Duccio
- Rooftop café (Duomo views)

**Time Needed:** 2.5-4 hours (depends on stamina)

---

## Late Afternoon: Ponte Vecchio & River Walk

### After Uffizi (5-6 PM)

**Ponte Vecchio:**
- Medieval bridge (1345) with jewelry shops
- Walk across, browse shops (expensive!)
- Views of Arno River
- Only Florentine bridge Nazis didn't destroy in WWII

**Oltrarno Stroll:**
- Cross bridge into Oltrarno neighborhood
- Less touristy, artisan workshops
- Window-shop leather, antiques
- Rest tired feet at café

---

## Evening: Piazzale Michelangelo Sunset

### Sunset Views (7:30-9 PM)

**Getting There:**
- **Walk:** 20-25 min uphill from Ponte Vecchio
- **Bus:** #12 or #13 from station area
- **Taxi:** ~€10

**At Piazzale Michelangelo:**
- Panoramic Florence views
- Bronze copy of David statue
- Sunset over city (~8:30 PM in June)
- Street vendors, gelato cart
- Bring camera!

**Time:** Arrive 30 min before sunset, stay through golden hour

---

## Dinner (9-10 PM)

### Back in City Center

**After Sunset:**
- Walk or bus back down
- Oltrarno dinner or centro storico

**Restaurant Suggestions:**
- See [Restaurants page](../research/restaurants.qmd#florence-restaurants)
- Reserve if popular spot
- Or ask hotel concierge

**What to Try Tonight:**
- Pappardelle al cinghiale (wild boar pasta)
- Ribollita (Tuscan soup)
- House Chianti wine

---

## Reservations & Bookings

- [ ] Duomo dome climb (timed entry booked?)
- [ ] Uffizi Gallery (timed entry booked?)
- [ ] Dinner reservation (if needed)

---

## What to Bring

- [ ] Comfortable shoes (10,000+ steps today!)
- [ ] Water bottle (refill at fountains)
- [ ] Sunscreen, hat, sunglasses
- [ ] Camera (fully charged)
- [ ] Light jacket (museums can be cool)
- [ ] Scarf (shoulders covered for cathedral)
- [ ] Snacks (energy for museum marathons)
- [ ] Museum confirmations (printed or phone)

---

## Notes & Reflections

**Duomo dome climb:**

**Uffizi highlights:**

**Favorite artwork:**

**Energy level:**

**Steps walked today:**

---

**Previous:** [Day 2](day-02-2026-06-05-pisa-to-florence.qmd) | **Next:** [Day 4](day-04-2026-06-07-florence.qmd)
